svg.style: background-color: #ffffff; font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; user-select: none;

g.text: Hovering a helicopter is like balancing on a beach ball. The fuselage acts as a pendulum beneath the rotor.
Pilots must constantly manage the Center of Gravity, balance anti-torque pedals, and correct for translating tendency.
Controls Tail Rotor Thrust to counter torque.
Tilts the rotor disk to move or compensate.
Tail rotor thrust pushes the craft sideways.
Pilots apply opposite cyclic, tilting the lift vector to counter it.
The fuselage hangs from the rotor hub.
If CG moves, the nose dips or rises.
Too far, and cyclic cannot tilt enough to save it.
